untranslatable_reason: This signal describes an options ratio calendar spread strategy with specific parameters (near-term sell quantity, longer-term buy quantity, strike selection, minimum credit). These are portfolio construction rules requiring multi-leg options pricing and greeks (delta, theta, vega) which are not available in the allowed indicator set. The signal cannot be expressed as a pure price/technical indicator-based rule.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_156 is an advanced options strategy that requires: (1) options chain data and leg-by-leg construction, (2) credit calculation across multiple legs, (3) dynamic quantity ratios based on premium, (4) strike selection logic relative to underlying price. A backtest engine using only price and technical indicators cannot properly model this strategy. To implement this signal, the backtest engine would need to support options Greeks, time decay modeling, multi-leg position sizing, and implied volatility term structure analysis.
